conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start by reading the official guidance on eligibility.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your inquiry. To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children that you are responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have three children - Alex, Blake, and Charlie. I'm interested in finding out if I can claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's helpful to know you have three children - Alex, Blake, and Charlie.

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for each of them, I need to ask a few questions about your circumstances.

First, do you currently live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Now I need to understand a bit more about each of your children.

What are the ages of Alex, Blake, and Charlie?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is 10, Blake is 19, and Charlie is 2.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Now I need to understand a bit more about Blake, who is 19 years old.

Is Blake currently in approved education or training? (For example, full-time non-advanced education such as A-levels, or approved training courses)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake isn't in any education or training at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. Now I need to ask about your living arrangements with each child.

Do all three children - Alex, Blake, and Charlie - currently live with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex and Blake live with me, but Charlie doesn't live with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I need to understand more about Charlie's situation since Charlie doesn't live with you.

Are you currently contributing financially towards Charlie's upkeep (such as for food, clothes, pocket money, or financial contributions for somewhere to live)? And if so, how much do you contribute?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not currently contributing financially towards Charlie's upkeep.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me also check a couple more things to complete the assessment.

Has anyone else claimed Child Benefit for any of your children - Alex, Blake, or Charlie?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final question to complete my assessment:

Has Blake (who is 19) started paid work for 24 hours or more a week, or is Blake claiming any benefits in their own right such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake hasn't started working 24 hours or more a week, and isn't claiming any benefits in their own right.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all the information. Based on the UK government's Child Benefit eligibility rules, here is my assessment for each of your children:

**Alex (10 years old) - ELIGIBLE ✓**
You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex. Since Alex is under 16, lives with you in the UK, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them, you meet all the requirements.

**Blake (19 years old) - NOT ELIGIBLE ✗**
Unfortunately, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Blake. While children under 20 can qualify if they are in approved education or training, Blake is not currently in any education or training. Child Benefit for children over 16 only continues if they remain in approved education or training.

**Charlie (2 years old) - NOT ELIGIBLE ✗**
Unfortunately, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Charlie. Although Charlie is under 16, Charlie does not live with you. To be eligible when a child doesn't live with you, you would need to be contributing at least the same amount as the Child Benefit payment (or equivalent in kind) towards Charlie's upkeep, such as for food, clothes, or pocket money. Since you are not making these contributions, you don't meet the responsibility requirements for Charlie.

**Summary:** You can claim Child Benefit for Alex only. If circumstances change in the future (for example, if Blake enters approved education or training, or if you start contributing to Charlie's upkeep), you may wish to reassess your eligibility for those children.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 10 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 19 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 2 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 19 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 2 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 8 weeks (within 12-week limit)
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 10 years old and lives with you in the UK. According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. You'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. Since Alex is under 16, lives with you, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them, you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 19 years old and lives with you. According to the eligibility rules, you'll normally still qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. However, Blake is not currently in any approved education or training. Since Blake is over 16 and not in approved education or training, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Blake.

Charlie is 2 years old and does not live with you. According to the eligibility rules, you'll usually be responsible for a child if either: you live with them, or you're paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or the equivalent in kind) towards looking after them - for example on food, clothes or pocket money. Since Charlie does not live with you and you are not contributing financially towards Charlie's upkeep, you do not meet the responsibility requirements. Therefore, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Charlie.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You have three children: Alex (10), Blake (19), and Charlie (2). You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex only, as Alex is under 16 and lives with you. You are not eligible for Blake because, although Blake lives with you, Blake is 19 and not in approved education or training. You are not eligible for Charlie because Charlie does not live with you and you are not making financial contributions towards Charlie's upkeep.
